Missile attack at the central square killed 10 people and wounded 35. In total, over 50 people were wounded and 29, including three children and one Indian student, were killed. 87 houses were ruined, including the consulate of Slovenia.

Around 1 March 2022 (month of), Russian forces shot small arms at a civilian car with a family trying to evacuate near Novyi Burluk, having wounded a 10-year-old child. When the family tried to get medical help near Bazaliyivka, Kherson, a different group of Russian soldiers shot at them again. The child was killed, her grandfather and grandmother were injured.

Around 1 March 2022 (beginning of month), Russian forces opened fire on a family of four as they drove on two civilian cars on the 'Zhytomyr highway' near Severynivka, Kyiv. They were trying to evacuate from the occupied Severynivka. The couple was killed immediately and a 15-year-old child received a gunshot wound. The Russian soldiers took two sisters to their IFV. The younger (8-year-old girl) was forcibly taken to a man who lived near the location of the Russian unit. The older child (15-year-old) was taken outside of Ukraine. Only nine months later, it became known that the older sister also died of her gunshot wound. The death of the 15-year-old girl was confirmed by a forensic medical examination. The child's body was returned to Ukraine. Furthermore, according to law enforcement officials, on the same day, the same Russian soldiers fired on another car with firearms (at an unspecified location). The car was carrying a driver and four passengers, but there were no casualties, only their car was damaged.
